taxonomy.enforced: Runtime hook/tool-call guard is verified and tested. Smoke test or interactive test exists.
taxonomy.verified-plugin-wired: Native plugin artifacts are shipped and smoke-tested, but runtime enforcement is not proven.
taxonomy.plugin-wired: Native plugin artifacts exist but lack smoke tests.
taxonomy.verified-wired: Instruction/pointer adapter files, install/update wiring, generated pointer files, and config examples are validated by smoke tests, but runtime enforcement is not proven.
taxonomy.wired: Files/config/install paths exist and are validated. Runtime enforcement may not be proven yet.
taxonomy.documented: Documentation exists, but no verified wiring or runtime enforcement.
taxonomy.planned: Roadmap item exists, but no shipped adapter wiring yet.

adapters.name: Pi
enforcementSurfaces: tool_call hook (command blocking, file path blocking)
limitations: Not a sandbox; best-effort local classification
Does not replace host permissions or sandboxing

adapters.name: AXGA
enforcementSurfaces: Shares Pi extension; enforcement depends on host-compatible hooks
limitations: No proof of runtime enforcement in AXGA specifically

adapters.name: Codex
enforcementSurfaces: Workspace AGENTS.md pointer file created by installer
Native plugin manifest with skills, hook config, and marketplace manifest
Live-verified: real `codex plugin marketplace add` + `plugin add` install/trust against the actual Codex CLI (isolated CODEX_HOME)
Live-verified: real `codex exec` turn denies `git push` and a `.env` write via the PreToolUse hook (isolated CODEX_HOME, `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust`)
limitations: Live proof requires `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust`; the normal interactive hook-trust prompt flow is not separately verified
Guard rules only cover git push and .env-style writes today
Pointer adapter remains the fallback path
Plugin skill surface is heli-governance + heli-target only; Pi/AXGA ships 23 skills, this plugin does not

adapters.name: Claude Code
enforcementSurfaces: Workspace CLAUDE.md pointer file created by installer
Native plugin manifest with skills and hook config
Live-verified: real Claude Code session (`--plugin-dir`, isolated sandbox repo) denies `git push` and `.env` writes and reports them in the session's `permission_denials`
limitations: Live proof used `--plugin-dir` (session-scoped local plugin loading), not the marketplace-installed-and-trusted flow (`claude plugin install`); that install/trust path is not separately verified
Guard rules only cover git push and .env-style writes today
Pointer adapter (CLAUDE.md) remains the fallback path for hosts that don't load the native plugin
Plugin skill surface is heli-governance + heli-target only; Pi/AXGA ships 23 skills, this plugin does not

adapters.name: Cursor
enforcementSurfaces: Workspace .cursorrules or .cursor/rules/ pointer created by installer
limitations: Relies on Cursor reading adapter file voluntarily
No smoke test proving agents read the pointer file
